narrative: Markets enter the session in a cautious wait-and-see posture as the Fed decision looms, with risk-off sentiment pervading flows despite a technically risk-on medium regime — a notable contradiction worth monitoring. USD consolidates with a neutral bias, yet technicals rank it the strongest currency at 100, suggesting the path of least resistance remains higher once the Fed catalyst clears. USD/JPY commands attention as daily signals turn bullish with price pinned below the 160 intervention threshold; BoJ's hawkish hold reinforces divergence that technicals are beginning to confirm. A sharp contradiction emerges in CHF, where news flow highlights persistent safe-haven strength and USD/CHF trading below 0.7900, yet technicals rank CHF dead last at zero with H4 buy signals on USD/CHF — favouring a technical bounce higher in the pair. AUD shows resilience on hot CPI with strong technical ranking at 92, though broader risk-off and USD firmness cap AUD/USD upside. **Actionable watch point: USD/CHF on an H4 buy setup — a clean break above 0.7900 with Fed-driven USD momentum would confirm the technical signal over the safe-haven narrative.**

themes.theme: Fed rate decision dominance – markets in wait-and-see mode with USD consolidating
Oil supply shock – US extending Iran blockade, UAE exit reshaping supply, WTI near $97 lifting energy-sensitive pairs
Central bank policy divergence week – Fed, BoE, ECB, BoC all in focus with rates expected steady across the board
JPY cautious ahead of Fed with BoJ hawkish hold reinforcing rate path expectations; USD/JPY pinned below 160 intervention threshold
Hot Australian CPI lifts AUD/NZD to long-term highs but AUD/USD pressured by broader USD and risk sentiment
CHF strength persists – USD/CHF below 0.7900 reflecting safe-haven demand amid geopolitical and inflation concerns
EUR range-bound around 1.1700 – Spanish CPI undershoot and German state CPI uptick send mixed signals ahead of ECB
GBP subdued in tight ranges ahead of Thursday's BoE – EUR/GBP flat above 0.8650
Gold vulnerable below $4,600, silver elevated above $73.50 – precious metals await Fed for directional cue
Rising oil prices and inflation concerns weigh on INR – USD/INR approaching all-time highs
Tech-led equity pullback on AI concerns and surging crude amplifies risk-off undertone

key_observation: Markets are in a holding pattern ahead of the Fed decision, but the dominant macro driver is the escalating oil supply shock (WTI ~$97, Iran blockade extension, UAE exit) which is feeding through to inflation fears (silver inflation shock, German CPI uptick), pressuring energy-importing EM (INR at record weakness), and reinforcing a mild risk-off tone via equity declines and safe-haven flows into CHF and JPY.
watch: Fed policy statement and dot plot guidance – any hawkish lean due to oil-driven inflation could break the USD out of consolidation to the upside; USD/JPY 160.00 intervention threshold remains the key technical/policy line in the sand.
